Requested extractions (from the direct visit to https://github.com/python/cpython):
1. Primary programming language used: MISSING — the visit result did not include the repository metadata showing the primary language. The visit returned a redirect/content marker ("https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer") and a list of links, but no explicit language value was provided in the returned content.

2. Number of stars (approximate): MISSING — the visit returned a link to the stargazers page (https://github.com/python/cpython/stargazers) but did not include the numeric star count in the returned content.

3. License type: MISSING — the visit did not return the repository's license text or the license label (e.g., "PSF", "MIT", "Python-2.0", etc.). No license information was present in the provided content.

4. Brief description of the repository: MISSING — the visit did not return the repository description text (the short summary that normally appears under the repository name on GitHub).

Evidence / citations (from the visit result):
- The visit action URL: https://github.com/python/cpython
- Links returned by the visit (see list above), including the stargazers page: https://github.com/python/cpython/stargazers

What is missing and why I cannot provide the exact values from this visit:
- The visit result returned only a redirect/content marker ("https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer") and a list of links present on the retrieved page. It did not include the repository's rendered metadata (primary language, star count, license label, or description). Therefore I cannot extract exact values for those four required facts from the provided visit output.

Suggested next steps (actions you can ask me to perform):
- Revisit https://github.com/python/cpython and fetch the full page HTML rendering (so I can extract the language, star count, license label, and description), or
- Visit the explicit stargazers URL returned (https://github.com/python/cpython/stargazers) to obtain the star count, and the repository's LICENSE file path (e.g., https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/LICENSE) to obtain the license text — if you want, I can attempt those visits now.

Note: All statements above are based solely on the data returned by the direct visit action recorded in the merged results. No external assumptions or other web requests were used.
output.action_summary: I visited the requested URL (https://github.com/python/cpython). The visit returned a redirect/placeholder and a list of 20 links (including the repo stargazers page). The visit output did not include the repository metadata fields requested (primary language, star count, license, description), so those four items are missing from the provided evidence. Relevant URLs from the visit: https://github.com/python/cpython and https://github.com/python/cpython/stargazers. I can re-visit the repo or follow the stargazers or license URLs on request to extract the missing values.

merged_results.title: Normalize and verify extracted facts from CPython page

result.title: Normalize and verify extracted facts from CPython page

details.intent: Take the raw extraction results from the visit node and (1) normalize formats (e.g., convert '48.2k' to a clear approximate form), (2) cross-check that all required fields were captured, and (3) produce a final structured result with confidence notes and any fallback notes if a field was missing.
required_inputs: visit node output: raw values and verification_snippets
processing_steps: Confirm repository identity matches 'python/cpython'. If not, flag as mismatch.

Normalize stars_approx: keep the original display string and also produce a human-friendly phrase (e.g., 'about 48,200' if original indicates '48.2k'); if unable to convert, keep original display as authoritative.
Trim and summarize the description to one concise sentence (max ~25 words) using the page text as source.
Standardize license_type to the exact label seen on the page; if the page shows a link, use the link text.
Produce a confidence score for each fact (high/medium/low) based on presence and clarity of on-page evidence.
If any fact is 'NOT FOUND', include the captured context and suggest next steps.

output_fields.stars_approx_human: approx numeric phrase if convertible, else null
output_fields.notes: any anomalies or fields not found

details.rationale: Normalization and verification step ensures the raw scraped values are consistent, human-readable, and include confidence metadata to support downstream use or saving.

7301f9ff-abb1-41e9-94d4-80fa36908d55.title: Visit CPython GitHub repository and extract language, stars, license, description

details.intent: Directly fetch the repository page (mandated) and extract the four required facts: primary programming language, approximate star count, license type, and a brief repository description. Do not rely on search results.
extraction_plan: Load the repository page HTML and locate the repository title/header to confirm repository identity (python/cpython).
Locate the repository description text under the repo title/header and extract a concise one-sentence description.
Find the approximate star count visible near the 'Star' button (the stargazers count) and capture the displayed string (e.g., '48.2k').
Identify the primary programming language shown on the repository page (often in the language bar or the Languages section) and extract the top language name.
Locate the license indicator (often a link or label reading e.g. 'Python-2.0' or 'PSF License') and extract the license name as shown.
Capture short verification snippets (the exact page text lines or HTML snippets) for each extracted fact and include the page URL as source_url.

output_fields.verification_snippets: object mapping each fact to a short page excerpt for verification

verification_steps: Ensure the repository header shows 'python/cpython' to confirm correct page.
For stars, record the exact visible label (if abbreviated with 'k', keep abbreviation) and note the DOM text for traceability.
For license, prefer the explicit license badge/label found on the page (sidebar or repository meta).
For primary language, take the top listed language from the language bar or the first language in the Languages area.
If any target_fact is missing on the page, record 'NOT FOUND' and capture surrounding context for diagnosis.
details.rationale: This visit node directly satisfies the mandate to retrieve facts from the specified GitHub page. Extracting page text and verification snippets gives evidence to support each reported fact and reduces uncertainty by referencing the page itself.
